skill.name: zod-normalize-inputs
skill.description: Normalizes untrusted stringly-typed or loosely typed inputs into domain-friendly values with Zod coercion, transform, and refine. Use when query/path params, form fields, DB row shapes, or similar boundary inputs need typed normalization before application logic. Do not use for HTTP JSON response validation, schema-variant derivation from a base schema, or inputs already normalized upstream.

authoring.singleJob: Define one reusable skill that normalizes untrusted boundary inputs with Zod so downstream code can rely on typed values without repeating normalization across layers.

interface.short_description: Normalize untrusted inputs with Zod coercion, transform, and refine for params, forms, DB rows, and similar boundary values.
interface.default_prompt: Use this skill when inputs arrive as strings or loosely typed values at a boundary and need one-time normalization with Zod before business logic.

shouldTriggerWhen: A task needs to normalize query params or path params from strings into typed values with Zod
A task needs to normalize form field values into domain-friendly types before application logic
A task needs to decode DB rows or other stringly shaped records into typed values with Zod
A task needs to transform validated boundary strings into runtime values such as Date
A task needs refine or superRefine rules on top of normalized boundary inputs
shouldNotTriggerWhen: The main task is validating HTTP JSON responses or API payloads coming from external services
The main task is deriving create, patch, public, or private schema variants from an existing base schema
The input values are already normalized and validated upstream
The task is general TypeScript coercion cleanup without adopting or extending Zod boundary schemas
stopConditions: It is unclear whether normalization belongs at this boundary or has already happened in another layer
The request mixes normalization with response validation or schema-variant derivation as one inseparable task
The boundary source of the untrusted values is missing or ambiguous
The task would require duplicating the same normalization logic across multiple layers instead of choosing one boundary

outcomeGoals: The skill routes boundary normalization tasks to Zod coercion, transform, and refine patterns
The skill keeps normalization as a one-time boundary concern instead of repeated downstream coercion
boundaryGoals: The skill cleanly excludes HTTP JSON response validation
The skill cleanly excludes schema-variant derivation from base schemas
The skill stops when boundary ownership or upstream normalization is ambiguous
styleGoals: Pattern routing remains shallow and explicit
efficiencyGoals: The core workflow stays in SKILL.md while examples and pitfalls remain in references
The skill minimizes duplicate normalization guidance across layers
nonGoals: Implement the maintained skill package in this phase
Author eval coverage in this phase

activationProbes.prompt: Normalize Express query params with Zod so page and completed become typed values before filtering.
activationProbes.expectedSignal: Trigger for boundary input normalization with coercion and optional handling.

activationProbes.prompt: Take a submitted date string and normalize it to a Date with Zod before service logic uses it.
Decode a DB row where booleans arrive as 0/1 and timestamps as strings into a typed shape with Zod.
activationProbes.expectedSignal: Trigger for stringly DB row normalization.

comparisonFocus: Boundary normalization tasks that should trigger the skill
Nearby requests that belong to API response validation instead
Nearby requests that belong to schema variant derivation instead
Ambiguous cases where boundary ownership should force stop-and-ask
notes: This artifact freezes the contract only and does not implement the maintained skill files.
Later dogfooding should compare real contract-first use against the expected forge behavior and move any missing guidance into the forge skills rather than leaving it external to the normal flow.

negativeSignals: Validate a REST response body from fetch or axios with Zod
Derive CreateUserSchema and PatchUserSchema from a base object schema
Normalize values that are already typed and validated in an earlier boundary layer
Mix boundary normalization, response parsing, and schema derivation in one inseparable instruction
